_comment: Shared shadow lookup. Used by event handlers that produce a servant-of-Shadow encounter — primarily Lookout → Threats and Camp Events on the Eye of Sauron outcome. Filtered by region: orc-kind range widely but not into every realm; attercops belong to Mirkwood; wargs haunt the Misty Mountains and the Wild. Stat blocks are not reproduced; GMs refer to the core rulebook or relevant supplement.

shadow.name: Trolls
shadow.description: Stone-skinned, slow-witted, dangerous — trolls range at night and hide from the sun. A pair or a small party; one alone is already a grave threat, and trolls rarely walk without kin or master nearby.

shadow.name: Orcs
shadow.description: Orc-kind on the move — a scouting party, a patrol, or a full war-band. Swift in the dark, cruel in their ways, servants of the Shadow in its many forms.
shadow.stat_reference: Core rulebook — adversary statistics. Use Uruk-hai or Mordor Orcs from Hands of the White Wizard if appropriate to the region.

shadow.name: Wargs
shadow.description: Wolf-kin intelligent beyond animal reason, often bearing orc-riders. Yellow-eyed, cunning, hunting in packs. Where one warg walks, more follow.

shadow.name: Evil Men
shadow.description: Men of the East or the South, or kin turned to the Shadow's service — soldiers, emissaries, spies. A patrol in foreign livery, a rider with hidden purpose, or a raiding party from beyond the known lands.
shadow.stat_reference: Core rulebook — adversary statistics. Hands of the White Wizard for Dunlendings, Easterlings, and Haradrim of Rohan / Gondor campaigns.

shadow.name: Undead Spirits
shadow.description: Wights, barrow-wraiths, restless dead given form by Shadow — cold, ancient, hungry for the warmth of the living. The worst kind of threat, against which steel alone may not avail.

shadow.name: Attercops
shadow.description: Great spiders of Mirkwood, kin to the spawn of Ungoliant — eight-legged, venomous, cunning, with eyes that shine like cold lamps in the dim. Rarely found outside the great wood.
